email.subject: Daily Journey: Walker Miles on www.skechers.com

review.raw_markdown:
--- # Walker Miles — Skechers.com Mobile Journey Review **Date:** March 28, 2026 | **Yesterday's Score:** 4/10 --- ## 1. Executive Summary Well, I went through the whole thing again today on my phone and I'll be straight with you — it's still a bumpy ride. The site looks decent enough, found my Hands Free Slip-ins without too much trouble, but then hit a wall when it counted: the cart ended up completely empty after I tried to add a shoe. That's a deal-breaker. Feels like I went to the store, picked up a shoe, walked to the register, and they told me my basket was empty. The homepage still greeted me like I'm a woman shopping for wedges, even though I'm a logged-in Skechers Plus member. Some things got a tiny bit clearer in the navigation, but the fundamental problems are still there. --- ## 2. Business Impact Score: 4/10 **Same score as yesterday. No meaningful improvement. Core conversion path remains broken.** --- ## 3. What's Working - **BOGO 50% OFF is front and center** — that big promotional banner at the top and the sticky bar at the bottom registered clearly. Good visibility on the deal, I noticed it right away. - **"Hands Free Slip-Ins" is a direct link in the Men's menu** — once I tapped MENU and went to Men's, I could see "Hands Free Slip Ins" listed right there without digging. That's a win. Saved me from hunting. - **Product page is readable** — the Azusah product page had a big clear price ($135.00), decent-sized size grid buttons, and the ADD TO CART button was prominent. Text was large enough that I didn't have to squint. - **258 results on the Slip-ins page** — solid selection. Good to see there are lots of options. - **Free pickup callout on category page** — "Free pickup at 364 locations" was a nice touch for someone like me who might want to try them on first. - **BUY 1 GET 1 50% OFF shown on product page** — reminded me the deal applies at cart, which I appreciated. --- ## 4. What's Weak - **Cart is empty — the add-to-cart completely failed.** This is the same critical bug as yesterday. I went through the whole journey, got to the cart, and it said "There are no items in your cart." That's a total conversion failure. This is the #1 problem on this site right now. - **Homepage defaults to Women's — again.** Even after logging in, the first thing I saw was women's shoes. I'm a 62-year-old man who's bought Skechers before. Why am I looking at the Hotshot Kickoff Women's shoe as the hero product? That's not me. - **The email popup hits immediately.** First thing I'm trying to do is look at the site and a "Unlock 20% OFF" modal slaps me in the face. I'm already a Skechers Plus member — why are you trying to get my email? Feels tone-deaf. - **Login didn't appear to personalize anything.** Step 3 showed the same homepage I saw before logging in. No "Welcome back, Walker" — nothing. The logged-in state made zero visible difference. - **Add to cart required size selection but didn't communicate it well enough.** Step 7 shows "Please select a size" — that's fine as validation goes, but the message was small and I had to hunt for what went wrong. Could have been clearer upfront. - **Search for "slip in" returned 13,157 results** — that's overwhelming. And the results were mixed men's and women's with no automatic filter for my gender even though I'm logged in as a man. A 62-year-old isn't going to page through 13,000 results. - **0 reviews on the product page.** The star rating widget shows up but says "0 Reviews." That erodes my confidence. If nobody's reviewed it, how do I know if it's comfortable for older feet? --- ## 5. Recommendations 1. **Fix the cart bug immediately.** This is a revenue-killing defect. Every session that ends with an empty cart is a lost sale. Priority one, no debate. 2. **Personalize the homepage for logged-in members.** If I'm signed in and my purchase history is Men's Slip-ins, default my homepage to Men's — or at least remember I tapped Men's last time. A/B test a "Welcome back" hero for Plus members. 3. **Suppress the email popup for logged-in Plus members.** I'm already in the loyalty program. Asking me for my email is pointless and annoying. Show me my points balance or a member-exclusive deal instead. 4. **Filter search results by gender for logged-in users.** If I search "slip in" while logged in as a male, default the results to Men's. Let me opt into seeing women's if I want. 5. **Make size selection more obvious before the button.** Put a clear "Choose your size" prompt above the ADD TO CART button with better visual weight — not just a small error after I tap. 6. **Surface reviews or social proof on the product page.** If a product has no reviews, show "Be the first to review" or pull in ratings from similar products. An empty review widget makes the shoe look new and untested. --- ## 6. Bottom Line I'm a loyal Skechers Plus member who came to this site ready to buy a $135 pair of Slip-ins and left with an empty cart and no purchase. The navigation to find the shoe was actually decent today — the Men's menu with Hands Free Slip-Ins as a direct link was helpful — but everything after that fell apart. The site doesn't know who I am even when I'm logged in, and the broken cart makes all of that irrelevant anyway. **Score: 4/10 — No change from yesterday. The browsing experience is tolerable but the purchase experience is broken.** --- ## 7. Evidence ### Step 1 — Homepage What I saw: A Spring Shopping Guide banner with a multigenerational family photo — nice, relatable imagery. But the product grid below defaulted to **Women's** shoes (Hotshot - Kickoff Women's, UNO Wedge Women's, Hotshot - Blooming Blossoms Women's). Women's tab was underlined as active. Men's and Kids' tabs visible but not selected. BOGO 50% OFF banner at top. MENU button visible top right. - Phone usability: **Acceptable.** Big hero, easy to see. - Friction: Yes — I'm a man and I'm looking at women's shoes as the default. Minor irritation. - Personalization: **None.** Felt like I'd never been here before. --- ### Step 2 — Dismiss Popups What I saw: A full modal overlay appeared with "Skechers — Unlock 20% OFF — PLUS, BE THE FIRST TO KNOW ABOUT SPECIAL OFFERS!" with an email field and CONTINUE button. X button in top right to close. - Phone usability: **Manageable.** The X to close was visible and tappable. - Friction: **Yes — immediate interruption.** I hadn't even seen the homepage yet. - Personalization: **Completely impersonal.** I'm a Plus member being asked to sign up for emails. --- ### Step 3 — Log In What I saw: The homepage again — same layout as Step 1. No login screen captured in the viewport; appears login happened silently or via a different path. - Phone usability: N/A — no visible login form in this screenshot. - Friction: **Yes — no visible confirmation I was logged in.** No welcome message, no name displayed. - Personalization: **None visible.** The page looked identical to the pre-login state. --- ### Step 4 — Men's Category What I saw: A right-side slide-out drawer labeled "MEN" with menu items: Shoes, Shop by Activity, Comfort Technologies, Collections, Collaborations, Clothing & Accessories, **Hands Free Slip-Ins** (direct link!), Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit, Men's Sale, New Arrivals, Best Sellers, Spring Shopping Guide. - Phone usability: **Good.** Items were spaced reasonably, text was readable, chevrons indicated submenus. - Friction: Low. "Hands Free Slip-Ins" was right there — didn't have to hunt. - Personalization: **Neutral.** Standard menu, nothing tailored to my history. But the direct link to my category of interest was a practical win. --- ### Step 5 — Men's Shoes (Hands Free Slip-ins) What I saw: Category page for "Hands Free Slip-ins." Tagline explained the Heel Pillow technology. Filter chips across the top: Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit, Medium Fit, Black, Casual, Athletic, Work, Arch Fit. 258 results shown. Free pickup at 364 locations. First row showed three shoes including the Azusah ($135.00). BUY 1, GET 1 50% OFF sticky bar at bottom. - Phone usability: **Good.** Product cards were a reasonable size. Filter chips tappable. - Friction: Minor — 258 results is a lot to browse on a phone with no default sort that helps me narrow down. - Delight: The technology description was helpful — I like knowing what "Heel Pillow" means. - Personalization: **Slight.** The page is at least in my right category. --- ### Step 6 — View Product What I saw: Skechers Slip-ins: Max Cushioning Arch Fit 2.0 - Azusah. $135.00. Star rating with "0 Reviews" — the widget showed but no actual reviews. Color swatch (White/Gray selected). Width: Medium. Size grid from 7.0 to 13.0+. Large ADD TO CART button. "Slip It" delivery option. "Buy 1 Get 1 50% off Applied at Cart" note. - Phone usability: **Good.** Price was prominent. Size grid was tappable. Button was large. - Friction: **0 Reviews was off-putting.** I like to know other people's experience before spending $135. - Personalization: **None.** No "Based on your past purchases" or loyalty member note. --- ### Step 7 — Add to Cart What I saw: Size selection view with "Please select a size" validation message. Full size grid (7.0–14.0) visible. ADD TO CART button below. Skechers+ join prompt visible ("Join for Free Shipping, Free Returns, & Earn a $5 Reward"). Product description text below. - Phone usability: **Mostly okay.** The size buttons were tappable. The error message was small. - Friction: **Yes.** The validation message wasn't obvious enough — easy to miss on a small screen. - Personalization: **Missed opportunity** — showing the Skechers+ join prompt to what should be a logged-in Plus member is a mistake. --- ### Step 8 — View Cart What I saw: "Shopping Cart" heading. Skechers Plus promo with JOIN NOW and SIGN IN buttons. **"There are no items in your cart."** Order Summary showing $0.00 across the board. CONTINUE SHOPPING button. Saved for Later (0 items). Recently Viewed section. - Phone usability: Page itself was clean and easy to read. - Friction: **Maximum.** The cart is empty. I did not successfully purchase anything. This is a complete failure of the conversion funnel. - Personalization: **The JOIN NOW / SIGN IN prompt suggests I appear logged out** — even if I logged in during Step 3, the cart doesn't recognize me as a Plus member. --- ### Step 9 — Search ("slip in") What I saw: Search results for "SLIP IN" — **13,157 results**. Sorted by Best Matches. First row showed: Skechers Slip-ins Arch Fit Arcade (Women's, $89.00), Skechers Slip-ins Contour Foam (Women's), Skechers Slip-ins GO RUN Glide-Step Mitus. Mixed men's and women's results, no automatic filtering to Men's despite being a logged-in male user. - Phone usability: **Adequate.** Product grid worked fine. Filter button visible. - Friction: **High.** 13,157 results with no gender pre-filter is overwhelming. Women's shoes appearing in my results is irrelevant noise. - Personalization: **None.** A system that knew me would default to Men's Slip-ins or at minimum lead with my most-relevant category. --- *Walker Miles | Skechers Plus Member | 62-year-old, comfort-first shopper* --- ## Technical Audit ## Technical Summary Platform: Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC), evidenced by `/on/demandware.static/Sites-USSkechers-Site/` paths. Bot protection via Kasada (`x-kpsdk-v`). Consent management via TrustArc. Tag management via GTM (`GTM-TDRBQZ`). Heavy third-party payload: Monetate, Quantum Metric, Bloomreach, Stylitics, iGoDigital, plus 10+ ad/tracking pixels. 37 axe violations detected across the journey; 2 broken third-party integrations. --- ## Accessibility **Critical (must fix):** - `image-alt` — `` elements missing `alt` attributes on Homepage and Men's Shoes. Screen readers get no product context. - `aria-required-parent` (Homepage) / `aria-required-children` (View Product) — ARIA role hierarchy broken; assistive tech will misinterpret widget structure. - `button-name` (View Product) — Icon-only buttons with no accessible label. Likely size/color selectors or carousel controls. **Serious:** - `link-name` (Homepage, Men's Shoes) — Anchor elements with no text or aria-label; probably image links or icon links. - `link-in-text-block` (Men's Shoes) — Links differentiated only by color in body text, fails WCAG 1.4.1. - `aria-dialog-name` / `aria-input-field-name` (View Product) — Modal dialogs and form inputs missing accessible names; likely size selector or popup overlays. **Moderate:** - `landmark-no-duplicate-main` / `landmark-main-is-top-level` (Homepage) — Multiple `
` elements detected. Indicates injected content (Monetate, widgets) adding its own landmark structure, conflicting with page `
`. - `landmark-unique` / `landmark-complementary-is-top-level` — `
` elements nested inside other landmarks instead of at document root. - `region` (Homepage, Men's Shoes) — Page content exists outside any landmark region; skip-navigation and screen reader region browsing incomplete. **Total reported: 37 violations across the journey; 17 additional not listed above.** --- ## SEO - **Placeholder GTM property ID:** `G-1234567890` appears in `
